problem: For a set-user-ID-root executable that also has a security.capability xattr, this treats exec_euid == 0 as making the file effective bit enabled even when the xattr's effective flag is clear. That bypasses the setuid-root-with-file-capabilities exception: a non-root caller executing such a file receives all newly permitted file capabilities in its effective set, although the file requested permitted-only capabilities.
fix: Honor the xattr effective flag for the setuid-root-plus-file-capabilities case. For example, exclude the root fast path when file capabilities are present and the old real UID is non-root, then fall back to FileCapabilities::has_effective_flag.
